WANNA ION SEOO AnC 0OCO .


The members of the Carnation
club were guests yesterday after
noon of Mrs. j. A. Maxwell at the
Maxwell home, on York avenue.


The decorations were of carna-
tions and the appointments at the
luncheon table We're carried out
with the same flowers. Luncheon
was served at | o'clock and covers
were laid for 80 guests.


The remainder of the afternoon
was spent with music and socially.


CIRCLE MEETS THURSDAY.


Circle Number 5 of the First
Methodist Episcopal church will
meet Thursday afternoon with Mrs.
A. G. Wilson at her home, 1873
Waverly place.


WOMAN'S CLUB MEETS.


The domestic science department
of the Woman's club held a meeting
this afternoon at 2:30 o'clock at
The North Central high school. Miss
Anna Rogers is instructor and Mrs.
Lucy b. Hall acted as chairman.
They took for their subject, Care
of the Sick and the Sick Room"
Mr.. AND MRS. DUNN ARE SUR.
PRiSED.


Reverend and Mrs. M. e. Dunn
